name: North Carolina (United States)
description: A vertical blue band at the hoist (one third of the flag's length) bearing a centred white five-pointed star flanked by gilt letters 'N' and 'C', with a scroll above (inscribed 'May 20th 1775') and a scroll below ('April 12th 1776'). The fly has two equal horizontal bands — red above white.
history: The first state flag was adopted in 1861 upon secession. The current design — modeled on the 1861 flag but with the colour order reversed and the dates updated — was adopted by the General Assembly on 9 March 1885. The May 1775 date refers to the contested Mecklenburg Declaration of Independence; the April 1776 date refers to the Halifax Resolves, the first official action by an American colony calling for independence.

features.symbolism: blue vertical band at the hoist (one third of the flag's length) with red over white horizontal bands on the fly — colours of the United States flag

features.role: the gilt letters 'N' and 'C' flanking the star
two scrolls above and below the star
features.symbolism: the upper scroll bears 'May 20th 1775' (the disputed Mecklenburg Declaration of Independence); the lower scroll bears 'April 12th 1776' (the Halifax Resolves authorizing North Carolina's delegates to vote for independence)
